ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "emotion_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the emotion associated with the tweet */,
  "emoVec" TEXT /* Emotion vector representing sentiment analysis results */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the person who tweeted */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tweet was created */,
  "embedded_text" TEXT /* Embedded text of the tweet itself */,
  "comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the tweet */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes received by the tweet */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets of the tweet */,
  "tweet_url" TEXT /* URL link to the tweet */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the tweet was posted */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used to post the tweet */,
  "is_retweeted" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the tweet is a retweet */,
  "retweet_original_tweet_id" INTEGER /* Original tweet ID if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "has_media" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the tweet contains media */,
  "media_urls" TEXT /* Array of URLs for any media attached to the tweet */,
  "hashtag_count" INTEGER /* Number of hashtags in the tweet */,
  "mentioned_user_count" INTEGER /* Number of users mentioned in the tweet */,
  "mentioned_users" TEXT /* Array of usernames mentioned in the tweet */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., Twitter Web App, Twitter for iPhone) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_username FOREIGN KEY ("username") REFERENCES users ("username"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_emotion_id FOREIGN KEY ("emotion_id") REFERENCES emotions ("emotion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language used by the user */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the user joined Twitter */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the user is following */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the user is verified */,
  "description" TEXT /* User's bio or description */,
  "website" TEXT /* User's website URL */,
  "tweets_count" INTEGER /* Total number of tweets the user has posted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "emotions" (
  "emotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each emotion */,
  "emotion_name" TEXT /* Name of the emotion (e.g., Joy, Fear, Disgust) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the emotion */,
  "color_code" TEXT /* Color code associated with the emotion for visualization purposes */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL of an icon representing the emotion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("emotion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement_metrics" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the tweet being engaged with */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet has received */,
  "comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the tweet */,
  "total_engagement" INTEGER /* Total engagement score (a combination of likes, retweets, and comments) */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate calculated as (likes + retweets + comments) / followers_count of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_metrics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the tweet associated with the keyword */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword associated with the tweet */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the keyword in the tweet */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score associated with the keyword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_keywords_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)
